name: Bank Of Erath
description_short: Bank of Erath is a community bank in Louisiana offering checking accounts, debit cards, mobile banking, and basic financial services with no online bill payment fees for internet banking.
description_long:
Bank of Erath is a community bank headquartered in Erath, Louisiana, operating since at least the time of this website's content. The bank maintains a physical presence with multiple locations and serves both individual and student customers in the region. Founded as a traditional brick-and-mortar institution, Bank of Erath has modernized its offerings to include digital banking options while maintaining its local community focus.

Bank of Erath offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, with specific programs for students featuring free checking with valid student ID and state ID. The bank provides internet banking with 24/7 account access at no charge, mobile banking through a dedicated app, and mobile wallet integration with Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Samsung Pay. Additional services include mobile check deposit, debit cards, ATM access, safe deposit boxes in multiple sizes, wire transfers, money orders, cashier's checks, certified checks, and reloadable gift cards. The bank also offers online bill payment for a $4.95 monthly fee and electronic statements.

Bank of Erath distinguishes itself through its emphasis on student banking with free accounts and no monthly fees, mobile-first convenience features like mobile deposit and mobile wallets, and transparent fee structures published on their website. The bank positions itself as a local, accessible alternative to larger national banks while adopting modern digital banking capabilities. Their multi-location presence in Louisiana suggests commitment to regional service and community banking relationships.

The bank's services are fairly standard for community banks, with some limitations worth noting. The $4.95 monthly online bill payment fee is an additional cost not all banks charge. Debit card usage carries a $2.00 monthly fee, which is less common at larger institutions. Foreign ATM fees are charged at $0.50 per inquiry or $2.00 per withdrawal, plus machine owner fees. The website provides limited information about loan products, credit products, or savings account rates, making it difficult to assess competitiveness in those areas. Bank of Erath appears best suited for students and local Louisiana residents seeking personal banking relationships rather than consumers requiring extensive loan products or investment services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Student checking accounts (free with valid student ID and state ID)
Mobile deposit (check deposits from mobile device)
Mobile wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay)
Safe deposit boxes (six sizes, $25 deposit required, $30-$200 annual rental)
Cashier's checks ($2 per $100, max $25 per check)
pros: Free checking accounts for students with valid student ID and state ID
No charge for internet banking with 24/7 account access
Mobile deposit allows check deposits from anywhere via mobile app
Supports three major mobile wallet platforms: Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Samsung Pay
No ATM fees when using Bank of Erath ATMs
Safe deposit boxes available in six different sizes with clear annual rental pricing
Transparent fee schedule published on website covering all major services
cons: $4.95 monthly fee for online bill payment service, which competes with banks offering this free
$2.00 monthly debit card fee plus $0.50 per foreign ATM inquiry or $2.00 per withdrawal in addition to machine owner fees
No information provided on website about loan products, interest rates, or savings account rates making comparison difficult
Limited service offerings compared to larger regional or national banks
Safe deposit box contents carry no insurance protection
best_for: College and university students in Louisiana seeking free basic checking accounts
Local Louisiana residents who value community banking relationships and in-person service
Customers comfortable with lower fee structures but accepting of modest monthly charges for premium features
Users of mobile banking and digital wallets seeking convenient account access
diagnosis: Bank of Erath is best for students and local Louisiana residents seeking accessible, transparent community banking with modern digital conveniences. The main caveat is that limited information about loan products, credit offerings, and account rates makes it difficult to assess overall competitiveness, and the $4.95 monthly bill payment fee and $2 monthly debit card fee may be higher than alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
